name: Chocolate-Glazed Triple-Layer Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 pkg chocolate wafer cookies, (8 oz) crushed
1/3 cup dark brown sugar, firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the cookie crumbs, 1/4 cup sugar, and melted butter; blend well. Press onto the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, combine 1 (8 oz) package cream cheese and 1/4 cup sugar. Beat until fluffy with an electric mixer.
3. Add 1 egg and 1/4 teaspoon vanilla to the mixture; beat well. Stir in melted chocolate and 1/3 cup sour cream. Spoon over the chocolate crust.
4. In another bowl, combine the remaining (8 oz) package cream cheese, brown sugar, and flour. Beat until fluffy with an electric mixer.
5. Add 1 egg and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla to the mixture; beat well. Stir in pecans. Spoon over the chocolate layer.
6. In a third bowl, combine 6 squares semisweet chocolate, 1/4 cup butter or margarine, 3/4 cup powdered sugar, 2 tablespoons water, and 1 teaspoon vanilla. Bring the water to a boil in a double boiler; reduce heat to low and cook until chocolate melts.
7. Remove from heat and stir in the remaining ingredients until smooth. Stir until the glaze is warm.
8. Bake at 325°F for 1 hour. Turn off the oven, leave it on, and keep the cheesecake in the oven for an additional 30 minutes with the door open.
9. Remove from the oven and let cool. Cover and chill for at least 2 hours. Remove from the pan and spread the warm chocolate glaze over the top of the chilled cheesecake. The glaze should not cover the entire surface but rather coat the top and run down the sides. Pour it over the cheesecake and spread to coat the sides as well.
10. Garnish if desired. Yield: 10 to 12 servings.
